name: Niall
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall saw a story about a house fire and asked over and over whether our smoke detectors work and if we'd all get out.
entries.parent_response: I checked the detectors with him three times to show him.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall asked repeatedly if he'd studied enough and what happens if he fails and gets held back.
entries.parent_response: I reassured him he'd be fine, over and over.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall wanted to know if the storm could be a tornado and kept checking the radar on my phone.
entries.parent_response: I let him check the app as much as he wanted.

entries.situation: I was 20 minutes late from work
entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall called me four times asking if I'd been in a car accident.
entries.parent_response: I answered every call and explained it was traffic.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall erased and redid his answers many times worried they were wrong; 20 minutes of work took 2 hours.
entries.parent_response: I sat with him and confirmed each answer was right.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall lists all his worries - school, money, whether grandpa is sick, climate change. Says he can't shut his brain off.
entries.parent_response: We talk through each worry until he's calm, sometimes until 11pm.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Niall worried he'd get hurt and asked if there'd be a nurse and whether I'd have my phone on.
entries.parent_response: I promised to keep my phone on and to pick him up if anything happened.

situations: Broad, shifting worry across domains (catastrophe/safety, performance/tests, weather, parent's safety, relatives' health, world events)
parental_responses: Participating in checking (detectors, radar, answering every call)
Talking through worries at length at bedtime
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct. GAD presentation is reassurance/checking-heavy rather than situational avoidance.
